_comment: Reference manifest for the GitHub App this action authenticates as. Use this as a checklist when filling in the 'Create GitHub App' form at https://github.com/settings/apps/new (or your org's equivalent). The future create-cla-action-config npx bootstrap script will submit this as a manifest POST so users can one-shot the App creation.
name: Self-Hosted CLA Assistant
description: Authenticates the Self-Hosted CLA Assistant GitHub Action so it can post PR comments and write the signatures file as a bot identity instead of a human-owned PAT.

_permission_notes.actions: write — re-run the failed CLA check after signing (pullRerunRunner.ts)
_permission_notes.contents: write — read + commit the signatures file (persistence.ts)
_permission_notes.issues: write — post/edit PR comments AND lock PR after merge (pullrequest/*)
_permission_notes.members: read — for `exempt-repo-org-members: true`; remove this line if you don't use that input (orgExemption.ts)
_permission_notes.pull_requests: read — read PR commits and committers via GraphQL (graphql.ts)

_event_notes.pull_request: fires on PR open/sync/close — matches the action's pull_request_target trigger
_event_notes.issue_comment: fires when contributors post the sign comment — matches the action's issue_comment trigger
_event_notes._webhooks_disabled: hook_attributes.active is false because this action never receives webhooks. The App is purely a token-minting facility for the GitHub Actions runtime.
